title: RFQ: Park Hill Park Design
agency: City and County of Denver Department of Transportation & Infrastructure
summary: Since at least 2018, neighbors have wondered what the future of the Park Hill Golf Club might be. Several different planning efforts or development proposals have engaged residents since that time, and in 2025, after the City of Denver purchased the land, Denver Parks and Recreation (“DPR”) launched a park visioning process. This process aimed to work with the community to develop a high-level vision framework of the important values, amenities, opportunities, and “bones” of the future park – the “big idea” to guide future design and construction phases. Work will generally include construction documents for demolition and site preparation; earthwork; drainage/channel improvements; site electrical including lighting, Ultraviolet (UV) treatment, and pumping system for water play area; paving & small pedestrian bridges; walls & boulders; site amenities; ecological and habitat educational features; shade structures; landscaping, including seeding, sodding, and native plantings; irrigation; irrigation pump station; outdoor adventure and playgrounds; and the potential to include 2- years of landscape-establishment. Vibrant Denver Bond Program : Approved by voters in 2025, the $950 million Vibrant Denver Bond Program is delivering 58 major projects across Denver, including libraries, parks and recreation centers, cultural facilities, affordable housing, improved streets and bridges, a family health clinic, a children’s advocacy center, and much more – without adding any new taxes. This bond program was informed by direct community input from thousands of Denverites and community leaders. The City will build the projects in this bond over six years from 2026 – 2031. A Pre-Submittal Meeting will be held on July 8, 2026, at 10:00 a.m., local time. title: Request for Unified Communications & Microsoft Teams Phone System
summary: REQUEST FOR QUOTATION Unified Communications & Microsoft Teams Phone System Clear Creek County, Colorado Issuing Department: Clear Creek County IT Department RFQ Number: RFQ #IT-2026-002 Issue Date: June 24, 2026 Submission Deadline: July 8, 2026 at 5:00 PM MDT Submission Method: Email only -- see Section 5 for instructions Contact / Submit To: For security reasons, you must enable JavaScript to view this E-mail address. Award Basis: Best overall value -- price is one factor among several 1. Purpose and Overview Clear Creek County IT Department is soliciting competitive quotations from qualified Unified Communications as a Service (UCaaS) providers for a Microsoft Teams-integrated phone system serving Georgetown, Colorado. This is an open public request; any qualified vendor may respond. The County operates an active Microsoft 365 tenant and seeks a provider to deliver Teams Operator Connect or Direct Routing-based telephony, cloud fax, call recording, SMS/text messaging, and associated hardware and implementation services. This RFQ is not a contract or a commitment to purchase. The County reserves the right to accept or reject any or all quotations, to waive any informalities, and to award to the vendor deemed most advantageous to the County. IMPORTANT: Award Notice -- Value Over Price Alone Vendors will NOT be selected solely on the basis of price. The County will evaluate proposals holistically, considering technical capability, service quality, implementation support, contract flexibility, references, and long-term value. Submitting the lowest price does not guarantee award. 2. Current Environment & Context Vendors should understand the County's existing environment when preparing proposals: - Primary location: Georgetown, CO 80444 - Approximately 233 users / DID lines requiring telephony services - Active Microsoft 365 tenant; Teams is the primary collaboration platform - Approximately 50 mobile users requiring Teams mobile integration - Fax requirements: approximately 10 cloud fax lines + 5 analog / MasterFax-style lines - Call recording required for approximately 3 users (compliance-grade) - SMS/text messaging required for approximately 20 Teams users - 2 conference / meeting room endpoints - Number porting required for approximately 233 US/CAN DIDs 3. Scope of Services Required Vendors must provide a complete solution covering all categories below. Partial proposals will not be considered unless the vendor clearly identifies which items involve third-party subcontracting and names the subcontractor. 3A. Teams Voice -- Operator Connect or Direct Routing - MS Teams Operator Connect (preferred) or Direct Routing -- US/CAN unlimited calling plan - Approximately 230 user seats, unlimited US/CAN inbound and outbound - 2 Resource Account Calling Plans (Auto Attendant / Call Queue) - 2 CT Cloud Voice / Basic SIP lines for non-Teams analog extensions - 3 active US DIDs (additional numbers beyond ported set) 3B. DID / Number Management & E911 - Porting of approximately 233 existing US/CAN telephone numbers - E911 surcharge and dynamic E911 compliance for all active lines - DID assignment professional services for MS Teams tenant configuration 3C. Cloud Fax - 10 cloud fax lines -- inbound and outbound, fax-to-email / email-to-fax - 5 analog / MasterFax-style unlimited lines with ATA adapters 3D. Call Recording - Compliance-grade call recording for approximately 3 users - Must include: unlimited audio retention, playback, tagging, AI transcription/summarization, and advanced admin licenses - Must be compatible with Microsoft Teams Compliance Recording API or equivalent 3E. SMS / Text Messaging for Teams - SMS/MMS standard license for approximately 20 Microsoft Teams users - Must support individual and shared inboxes, group messaging, and Teams-native notifications - 10DLC SMS campaign registration required 3F. Mobile Integration - Mobile background data or Teams mobile dialer solution for approximately 50 users 3G. Meeting Rooms & Webinar - 2 meeting room licenses (Zoom Rooms or Microsoft Teams Rooms equivalent) - Webinar platform supporting up to 1,000 attendees (annual license) 3H. Paging / Overhead Integration - 1 POE multicast paging adapter (e.g., ALGO 8301 or equivalent) for overhead paging integration 3I. Implementation & Professional Services - Full tenant setup, user provisioning, device configuration, and porting management - Auto Attendant and Call Queue configuration - Dedicated implementation project manager throughout deployment - End-user training: minimum 1 live session + post-go-live Q&A + on-demand admin module 4. Evaluation Criteria Proposals will be evaluated by a County review team using the following weighted criteria. Vendors are encouraged to address each area explicitly in their submission. Evaluation Category Weight Notes Technical capability and feature completeness 25% Meets all scope requirements Implementation plan, timeline, and project management 20% Dedicated PM, porting plan Ongoing support model and SLA (24/7 availability, response times) 20% Escalation paths, SLA terms Total cost of ownership (MRC, NRC, equipment, 1-year term) 20% Price is one factor among several References -- government / municipal client experience preferred 10% Provide 2-3 references Contract flexibility, auto-renewal terms, early termination provisions 5% 5. Submission Instructions Quotations must be submitted by email no later than July 6, 2026 at 5:00 PM MDT to: For security reasons, you must enable JavaScript to view this E-mail address. Email subject line must read: "RFQ #IT-2026-002 -- [Your Company Name]" Submissions must include all of the following: - Cover letter identifying your company, primary contact, and a brief overview of your proposed solution - Itemized pricing broken out by: Monthly Recurring Charges (MRC), Annual Recurring Charges (ARC), Non-Recurring / One-Time Charges (NRC), and Equipment costs (purchase or rent-to-own, clearly labeled) - Proposed 1-year contract term and any volume discount structures - Implementation timeline and methodology -- include porting process and estimated go-live date - Support model description: hours of availability, SLA response times, escalation paths, and dedicated account/success team details - 2-3 customer references -- preference given to government, county, or public-sector clients of comparable size - Any assumptions, exclusions, or technical prerequisites (e.g., Microsoft license requirements, GDAP access, network readiness) 6. Terms and Conditions - This RFQ is open for ten (10) calendar days from the issue date. - All pricing must be held firm for a minimum of thirty (30) days from the submission deadline. - The County is tax-exempt. A Colorado tax exemption certificate will be provided upon award. - Service location: 405 Argentine Street, Georgetown, CO 80444. - The County may negotiate with one or more vendors prior to award and reserves the right to request additional information, conduct demonstrations, or hold clarification interviews. - The County reserves the right to reject any or all quotations and to award based on best overall value as determined by the County's evaluation criteria. - Issuance of this RFQ does not obligate Clear Creek County to make a purchase or enter into a contract. 7. Questions All questions must be submitted via email to For security reasons, you must enable JavaScript to view this E-mail address. with the subject line "RFQ #IT-2026-002 -- Question." Questions and answers of general interest will be shared with all known interested parties. No questions will be accepted after July 6, 2026 at 5:00 PM MDT (two business days prior to the deadline). Issued by: Clear Creek County IT Department Contact: David Prows | 405 Argentine Street, Georgetown, CO 80444 | For security reasons, you must enable JavaScript to view this E-mail address.

If you need assistance, please contact BidNet’s Vendor Support Department at 800-835-4603, Option 2. Proposals shall contain a Cover Sheet, a Cost Proposal, signed by an authorized agent of the firm and, other detailed information listed below in the following paragraphs. The City of Steamboat Springs is soliciting bids from qualified contractors for all labor, material, and equipment necessary to clean and inspect via closed-circuit television (CCTV) about 89,000 linear feet of mainline existing sewers within the City of Steamboat Springs utility service area. The project will entail cleaning of all sections of sanitary sewer identified in the scope to include removal of debris and minor obstructions encountered within the mainline such as root balls, solids buildup, grease, and sediment and rocks to the greatest extent possible. summary: The Triangle Sitewide Designer will support design activities for the Triangle primarily focused on the Triangle site plan refinement and the horizontal infrastructure to support the remaining campus development including, GES Connections: Marion Underpass (Vibrant Bond), but this firm will also provide design collaboration and coordination for planned future facilities, including the proposed new Arena, Parking Garage, Central Utility Plant, Community Ownership / Affordable Housing, and Renovations to the 1909 Arena. Vibrant Denver Bond Program : Approved by voters in 2025, the $950 million Vibrant Denver Bond Program is delivering 58 major projects across Denver, including libraries, parks and recreation centers, cultural facilities, affordable housing, improved streets and bridges, a family health clinic, a children’s advocacy center, and much more – without adding any new taxes. This bond program was informed by direct community input from thousands of Denverites and community leaders. The City will build the projects in this bond over six years from 2026 – 2031. A Pre-Submittal Meeting will be held on July 7, 2026, at 12:00 p.m., local time. To access the meeting, please call 720-388-6219 and enter the conference ID: 404 334 924# or join via Microsoft Teams . Consultants are responsible for any information conveyed at this meeting. **Optional Site Visit: An optional site visit will be held on July 10, 2026, 9:00 a.m. - 11:00 a.m., local time . Please RSVP and the site visit details will be sent via email.

title: Linkage Fee Nexus Study
summary: The City of Wheat Ridge is soliciting proposals from qualified individuals, institutes, firms, or organizations to complete a Nexus Study for a Housing Impact (Linkage) Fee. The goal is to assess the demand for affordable housing generated by both residential and commercial development and to explore a legally defensible fee structure. The study should evaluate the potential impact on future development activity in Wheat Ridge and whether a linkage fee would be economically viable. This study will directly support the implementation of the City's Affordable Housing Strategy and Action Plan (AHS) by exploring a linkage fee as a secondary revenue source for the City’s Housing Fund. City Council provided this direction based on the results of a consultant-led Inclusionary Housing Zoning (IHZ) Feasibility Analysis, which indicated that an IHZ program may not be the most appropriate tool for Wheat Ridge at this time due to market constraints and development patterns.

All required State or Local demolition permits are the responsibility of the successful bidder. The property is located in Montrose County; all applicable permits would need to be through Montrose County. The cabin may be viewed by appointment on Monday June 22 nd between 9:00 am and 3:00 pm. All bids must be placed in a sealed envelope labeled Cabin Removal and submitted to Ouray County Administration at the Ouray County Courthouse, 541 Fourth Street, Ouray CO 81427, by Wednesday July 8 th 2026 by 3:00 pm. Bid award will not be solely based on highest bidder, but on best proposal period to expedite and complete the demolition and related material removal. Ouray County reserves the right to reject any and all bids or to accept any bid that in its judgment best serves the County. The successful bidder shall be required to comply with all Local, State and Federal requirements related to the project. Scope of work: The complete and total disassembly and removal of log cabin shell and all related building components, contents and materials down to the foundation. The recycling of any building materials possible is highly encouraged. Successful bidder is responsible for all disposal fees, permits and related costs for hauling away cabin shell and all remaining building materials. A base time of demolition must be included with the bid that clearly states time needed to complete the removal.

title: MEADOW LANE CULVERT REPLACEMENT
summary: The project consists of removal and replacement of an existing culvert with two upsized culverts and associated riprap. The work also includes, but is not limited to, sidewalk and asphalt removal and replacement and landscaping. Currently, the culvert is undersized for the 100-year storm event, creating the potential for overtopping, piping around the culvert, and eventual washout of the roads. The culvert crosses beneath Meadow Lane, a collector street in southeast Steamboat Springs, and conveys the flows of a tributary of Walton Creek known as the Bear Creek tributary. The project area is located within the Walton Creek watershed, and is Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) regulated and labeled as FEMA Zone A. Construction is anticipated in late August or early September of 2026, and is expected to last approximately six to eight weeks. Proposals must be submitted via the electronic submission on BidNet ( https://www.bidnetdirect.com/colorado/cityofsteamboatsprings)
